data.title: Morgan Sindall lifts 2026 profit outlook on strong Construction and Fit Out performance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Morgan Sindall ( (GB:MGNS) ).

Morgan Sindall Group has upgraded its outlook for 2026, stating that full-year profit before tax is now expected to be significantly ahead of previous expectations, driven mainly by strong performance and improved visibility in its Construction and Fit Out divisions. The group continues to show robust cash generation, with average daily net cash of £445m so far this year and full-year net cash expected to exceed £400m, supporting ongoing investment in partnership-focused growth.

In Partnership Housing, profit growth for 2026 is now expected to be modest amid subdued consumer sentiment, while Mixed Use Partnerships is on track with prior guidance and progressing multiple new schemes. Fit Out profits are forecast to be well above earlier expectations and to surpass the top end of its medium-term target, and Construction and Infrastructure are both anticipated to deliver operating margins at the upper end of their target ranges, underpinned by disciplined delivery, risk management, and strong order visibility.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:MGNS) stock is a Buy with a £5800.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Morgan Sindall stock, see the GB:MGNS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MGNS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MGNS is a Outperform.

The score is driven mainly by solid financial performance (consistent revenue growth, improved earnings, and manageable leverage), supported by a constructive longer-term technical trend. Valuation is reasonable with a moderate dividend, but thin margins and cash-flow volatility temper the overall rating.

To see Spark’s full report on MGNS stock, click here.

More about Morgan Sindall

Morgan Sindall Group is a UK-based construction and regeneration group operating through Partnerships, Fit Out, Construction, and Infrastructure divisions. The company focuses on affordable and mixed-use housing, office and commercial fit-out, and public and private sector construction and infrastructure projects, targeting long-term growth through capital-light frameworks and partnership models.

Average Trading Volume: 119,041

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £2.09B

Gaming Realms PLC has continued its share buyback programme, repurchasing 121,228 ordinary shares on 15 April 2026 at prices between 31.80 pence and 32.40 pence, with a volume-weighted average price of 32.14 pence. data.title: Audioboom Advances Strategic Review as Potential Bidders Consider Cash Offers
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Audioboom ( (GB:BOOM) ).

Audioboom Group has provided an update on its ongoing strategic review, launched in October 2025 to enhance shareholder value and accelerate growth by leveraging its position as a leading podcast publisher in the U.K. and U.S. The board, advised by J Goodwin & Co and Rockefeller Capital, has been assessing options including new investment for acquisitions, a substantial acquisition or reverse takeover, and potential disposals or a full sale of the company.

The company and its advisers have engaged multiple global media groups, sector peers and media-focused private equity firms, with several parties signing non-disclosure and standstill agreements and conducting due diligence. Audioboom is now in talks with a small number of potential buyers considering an all-cash offer for the entire share capital at indicative prices above the pre-review closing share price, though there is no certainty any offer will proceed or be recommended, and shareholders are being urged not to take action while the company targets concluding the review by 30 June 2026.

Spark’s Take on BOOM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BOOM is a Neutral.

Audioboom’s overall stock score is driven by strong technical indicators and positive corporate events, offset by challenges in cash flow generation and a high P/E ratio. The strategic review and record Q3 performance highlight potential growth opportunities, while the stock’s upward trend supports a positive outlook.

To see Spark’s full report on BOOM stock, click here.

More about Audioboom

Audioboom Group is a global podcasting company, operating a large-scale ad-tech and monetisation platform that supports commercial, distribution, marketing and production services for a premium roster of shows. Ranked as the fifth-largest podcast publisher in the U.S., it delivers around 135 million monthly downloads to more than 40 million unique listeners through partnerships across North America, Europe, Asia and Australia.

Average Trading Volume: 68,833

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £113.3M

data.title: Central Asia Metals Holds Output Steady and Accelerates Exploration Push
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Central Asia Metals ( (GB:CAML) ) is now available.

Central Asia Metals reported broadly steady first-quarter output, with Kounrad producing 2,880 tonnes of copper and Sasa delivering 4,282 tonnes of zinc-in-concentrate and 6,529 tonnes of lead-in-concentrate despite severe winter conditions. Management reiterated full-year 2026 guidance for 12,000–13,000 tonnes of copper, 18,000–20,000 tonnes of zinc and 26,000–28,000 tonnes of lead, suggesting operational performance remains on track.

At Sasa, the company signed a new collective bargaining agreement that locks in labour terms through early 2028, reshaped bonuses to drive productivity and cut headcount by just over 11% following a business review. The mine also continued improving orebody modelling and achieved its environmental objective of storing around 71% of tailings as dry stack or paste backfill, exceeding its 70% target.

CAML is stepping up exploration, finalising preparations for maiden drilling at its Yuzhnoe and Otyar prospects in Kazakhstan and advancing geophysics at Shaindy to refine copper targets. In parallel, it has secured an option over a sediment-hosted copper licence in the Tengiz Basin and expanded its licence footprint in the Chingiz-Tarbagatay belt, while Aberdeen Minerals has begun Phase 3 drilling at the Arthrath project in Scotland, with results expected in the third quarter of 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:CAML) stock is a Hold with a £190.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Central Asia Metals stock, see the GB:CAML Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CAML Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CAML is a Neutral.

The score is supported by a conservatively financed balance sheet and resilient cash generation, reinforced by management commentary on strong EBITDA/FCF and continued dividends. Offsetting this are elevated earnings volatility (including the impairment-driven loss) and clearly bearish technical signals with the stock trading below key moving averages.

To see Spark’s full report on CAML stock, click here.

More about Central Asia Metals

Central Asia Metals is an AIM-quoted UK resources group based in London, focused on base metals production and exploration. The company owns the Kounrad dump-leach SX-EW copper operation in Kazakhstan and the Sasa zinc-lead mine in North Macedonia, and also holds a 32.6% stake in Aberdeen Minerals’ Arthrath base-metals project in northeast Scotland.

Its portfolio is concentrated on copper, zinc and lead, with growing exposure to exploration-stage assets in Kazakhstan’s Chingiz-Tarbagatay belt and the Tengiz Basin. Through subsidiaries CAML X and CAML XD, the Group is pursuing copper and polymetallic discoveries, aiming to expand its reserves and diversify future production sources.

Average Trading Volume: 1,089,165

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £261.7M

Experian plc is a global data and technology group providing data, analytics and software that underpin lending decisions, fraud prevention, healthcare processes, digital marketing and automotive market insights. The FTSE 100-listed company serves clients in financial services, healthcare, automotive, agrifinance, insurance and other industries, and operates from Dublin with a workforce spanning 33 countries.

The company has repurchased 112,226 of its ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange as part of its ongoing share buyback programme announced earlier this year. The shares, bought through Goldman Sachs International at an average price of 2,684.0431 pence and to be cancelled, will reduce the company’s share count, supporting capital return to shareholders and potentially enhancing earnings per share.

Experian plans to publish details of all transactions executed under this repurchase programme on its website on a weekly basis, increasing transparency for investors and other stakeholders. The continued execution of the buyback underscores management’s confidence in the business and its cash generation, while signalling an active approach to balance sheet and capital structure management.

The FTSE 100 group operates across financial services, healthcare, automotive, agrifinance, insurance and other sectors, employing about 25,200 staff in 33 countries from its Dublin headquarters.

The company focuses on helping businesses and consumers make better financial decisions, save time and money, and manage risk through advanced data-driven solutions. data.title: Ashmore’s Assets Dip as Volatility Hits Emerging Markets, Japan Post Partnership Announced
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ashmore Group PLC ( (GB:ASHM) ) has provided an announcement.

Ashmore Group reported that assets under management declined 3% to an estimated $50.7 billion in the quarter to 31 March 2026, driven evenly by negative investment performance and net outflows. Fixed income strategies saw lower balances, particularly in blended and external debt, while local currency and equities recorded net inflows amid a challenging market backdrop.

The firm said emerging market performance was pressured by the widening conflict in the Middle East, which increased volatility and prompted some investors to pause allocations. Despite this, Ashmore highlighted continued benchmark outperformance across equity and fixed income strategies and announced a new strategic partnership with Japan Post Insurance, aimed at expanding access to a broad range of emerging market asset classes.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:ASHM) stock is a Sell with a £1.75 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ashmore Group PLC stock, see the GB:ASHM Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ASHM is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong profitability and an exceptionally low-leverage balance sheet, supported by constructive price momentum and an attractive income valuation (6.81% yield, P/E 14.343). The main constraints are declining revenue, weaker operating cash conversion, and earnings-call risks around performance-fee timing and gradual fee pressure.

To see Spark’s full report on ASHM stock, click here.

More about Ashmore Group PLC

Ashmore Group plc is a specialist emerging markets asset manager focused on fixed income, equities and alternative investment strategies. Frontier Developments ( (GB:FDEV) ) has provided an update.

Frontier Developments has continued to execute its previously announced share buyback programme, repurchasing 11,440 ordinary shares on 15 April 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of 384.08 pence per share through Peel Hunt. The company plans to hold these shares in treasury, bringing total shares bought back under the programme to 935,490 and leaving 36,024,518 voting shares in issue, a move that marginally enhances earnings per share and may signal management confidence while adjusting the free float for investors.

By reducing the number of voting shares in circulation, Frontier is subtly altering its capital structure and potentially improving per-share metrics for remaining shareholders. This ongoing buyback activity can also support the share price and underscores the company’s capacity to return capital to investors, which may be viewed as a positive signal regarding its cash generation and balance sheet strength in the competitive gaming sector.

Frontier Developments plc is a leading independent video game developer and publisher based in Cambridge, U.K. The company specialises in creative management simulation titles, leveraging its proprietary COBRA technology to build and sustain franchises such as Planet Coaster, Planet Zoo, and Jurassic World Evolution, with a strategy focused on long-term growth through strong player engagement and back-catalogue performance.
